zh.Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England: 英國斯塔福爾斯托可--特倫特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Vivian de Stoke於1150年出生在英國斯塔福爾斯托可--特倫特時，他的父親Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke58歲，母親Isolde de Brereton16歲。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1174年在英國英格蘭享年24歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stoke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places in England named with Middle English stoke (Old English stoc). The exact sense of the word is not clear, but it seems to have originally meant ‘place’ and was chiefly used for settlements dependent on a nearby estate center. Compare Stoker .

variants.name: Stoker
meanings: English: habitational name for someone who lived at a place called Stoke; see   Stoke  .
English: in northeastern England, a variant of   Stokoe  .
English: in southern England, especially in Sussex and Surrey, a variant of   Stocker  .

variants.name: Stokes
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Warwickshire): variant of Stoke with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. This name has been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

variants.name: Stowe
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Stow or Stowe, all named with Old English stow ‘place, holy place, assembly place’ (a word akin to stoc; see Stoke ). In a few cases the surname appears to be topographic, denoting someone who lived by a church or monastery, from Middle English stow(e) ‘holy place, church, monastery’. Places in Buckinghamshire, Cambridgeshire, Lincolnshire, Northamptonshire, and Staffordshire having this origin use the spelling Stowe, but the spelling difference cannot be relied on as an indication of locality of origin. The final -e in part represents a trace of the Old English dative inflection.
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 司徒, see   Situ  .

nameMeanings.name: Vivian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

en.lifeSummary: When Vivian de Stoke was born in 1150, in Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England, his father, Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke, was 58 and his mother, Isolde de Brereton, was 16. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1174, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 24.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Vivian de Stoke est né en 1150, à Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke, avait 58 ans et sa mère, Isolde de Brereton, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1174, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 24 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Vivian de Stoke 1150 in Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke 58 und seine Mutter Isolde de Brereton 16. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1174 im Alter von 24 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Vivian de Stoke è nato il nel 1150 nel Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke, aveva 58 anni e il suo madre, Isolde de Brereton, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1174, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 24 anni.

ja.Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England: イギリススタフォードシュアイアストケ-オン-トレント
ja.lifeSummary: Vivian de Stokeが1150年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアストケ-オン-トレントで生まれた時，父Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stokeは58歳，母Isolde de Breretonは16歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1174年にイギリスイギリスで24歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Vivian de Stoke(이)가 1150년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스토케--트렌트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke(은)는 58세였고, 그의 어머니 Isolde de Brereton(은)는 16세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1174년 영국 잉글랜드에서 24세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Vivian de Stoke nasceu em 1150, em Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke, tinha 58 anos e sua mãe, Isolde de Brereton, tinha 16 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1174, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 24 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Vivian de Stoke родился в 1150 г. в местности стоке-он-трент, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Isolde de Brereton, 16. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1174 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 24 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Vivian de Stoke nació en 1150, en Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Lord Gilbert of Manor Brunlee de Stoke, tenía 58 años y su mamá, Isolde de Brereton, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1174, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 24 años.
